title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at the crocodile and its ancestors that roamed Earth millions of years ago; how snakes rely on the heat of the sun to regulate their body temperature.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at the unique ways in which different animals avoid danger and protect themselves, from the viselike jaws of the hyena to the gunshot speed of the frog.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at how sea creatures have adapted methods of survival over time, from the deadly spines of a lionfish to the lightning speed of a barracuda.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: Examines how creatures protect themselves underwater and utilize an array of strategies just to stay alive.

title: Wild About
subTitle: The king of the jungle is on the brink of being a thing of the past, but for now it remains one of the last wild predators on earth.

title: Wild About
subTitle: Based on superstition and misconception, only intensive preservation efforts and increased public awareness may save them, but even then, some say it may be too late; until that day Sharks will continue to stalk waters everywhere.

title: Alaska's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: As this urban jungle continues to expand into the wilderness, encounters between people and wild Lynx are on the rise.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: As expert hunters, the Bald Eagle is perfectly adapted to life in tough arctic conditions, but in Anchorage, the secret to their survival...is trash.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: For years, at least four highly elusive wolf packs have lived and hunted on the edge of the wild city of Anchorage, Alaska; as the city spreads further and further into wolf country, the wolves are getting bolder.

title: Animal Science
subTitle: An animal series with a uniquely scientific approach shows how and why creatures are able to excel.

title: Animal Science
subTitle: In this episode we'll see the amazing strength of the leaf cutter ant. Find out how fast can a pronghorn really run, and how the desert camel is able to survive in such extreme conditions.

title: Rhino Orphanage
subTitle: Traumatized and lonely, Pemba is in need of a friend her own age.

title: Rhino Orphanage
subTitle: The Little Nyani is born a week premature, unable to keep up with her mother, she is sent to the rhino orphanage; raising a premature calf is a first for the orphanage.

title: Rhino Orphanage
subTitle: The poaching crisis intensifies and poachers turn their attention to orphanages; after losing her mother, four-week-old Jamila finally finds sanctuary at The Rhino Orphanage.

title: Rhino Orphanage
subTitle: The orphanage is full; cramped for space, the Hooligans inevitably begin to fight and Kalula is always in the thick of it; they desperately need a larger space.

subTitle: Desperately hungry elephants forage for food in farms, while the young calves begin to starve.

title: Echo and the Elephants of Amboseli
subTitle: The rains have brought life to Amboseli, saving the elephants from starvation.

title: Echo and the Elephants of Amboseli
subTitle: In Amboseli, nine months of unrelenting drought give way to longed-for rains; the elephants have enough to eat; Echo takes the family on a day out to the Snipe River.

title: Kalahari Meerkats
subTitle: The arrival of summer brings a large group of giraffes, which unsettles the Gosa gang and prompts them to make a move.

title: Kalahari Meerkats
subTitle: As the Gosa gang head back from the Whispering Forest, they run into a rival gang, and it's not long before full on war breaks out.

title: The Bear Whisperer
subTitle: The Bear Whisperer keeps the peace in the resort town Mammoth Lakes, California where tourists and bears collide.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Zamir's twins, Gangol and Ragin, cause mayhem on city streets.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Queen Rani disappears; tensions hit the boiling point; Galta Gang males challenge Zamir's authority.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Bipin, Yash and Tito go into the country to avoid the city monkey catcher.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: The monkey catches goes undercover; Bipin, Yash and Tito think they've found a new home.

subTitle: Tiger sharks in the Bahamas; relationship between sharks and remora fish.

title: Shark Dive TV
subTitle: Greenland Sharks in Canada's Gulf of St Lawrence; shark biology.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: From venomous death adders unseen in dried leaves to chameleons changing color in seconds to delicate weedy sea dragons blending with the gardens of the ocean, many animals are masters of the art of camouflage.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: From the mighty King Cobra to the lethal Black Widow Spider, a look inside the world of venomous animals and how they use their poisons.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: Of the quirky Australian pelican, the distinguishedly striped African zebra and the colossal whale that roams the great oceans, we explore which one is the mightiest migrator.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Marine naturalist and underwater filmmaker Jonathan Bird discusses animal themes, marine research, underwater exploration and recent discoveries.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Marine naturalist and underwater filmmaker Jonathan Bird discusses animal themes, marine research, underwater exploration and recent discoveries.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Jonathan Bird and Zack explore Belize's underwater world, showcasing local reef residents and blue fish.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Rasto the cargo carrying Kenyan camel gets a promotion; Mike the counterfeit currency sniffing dog fights crime in Washington; Rowdy the dog's unusual looks have made in a star in Hollywood; a five-year-old water buffalo in Thailand heads to school.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Ernie the Australian Shepherd.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Featuring performing sea lions.

subTitle: Zamir's twins, Gangol and Ragin, cause mayhem on city streets.

title: Second Chance Pets

subTitle: A dog named Draper suffers from amniotic band syndrome; a pit bull needs a knee brace after getting hurt playing.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: Rosie the pug is fitted with a prosthetic after being adopted from China; a dog named Bailey gets a brace.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Rod and Jessie are out of options with their aggressive Australian Cattle Dog, Cooper, whose animal instincts are causing him to herd humans, not cattle; the Dog Dudes must turn this cattle dog's aggressive behavior around.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Aria and Brixton are both fun-loving and friendly, until a traumatic Husky attack leaves Aria with a fear-aggression toward strange dogs; it's up to the Dog Dudes and their pack of Huskies to prove to Aria that Huskies aren't so bad after all.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Dog trainer brothers Dylan and Darijan boast seven years and hundreds of rehabilitated dogs under their collars; Dylan focuses on the dog, Darijan on the human psychology.

title: The Blue Realm
subTitle: At Mexico's Guadalupe Island, guests come face to face with great white sharks; in the Bahamas, scuba divers pay big bucks to get up close and personal with tiger sharks; shark advocates Dr. Erich Ritter and Chuck Anderson have grave reservations.

title: The Blue Realm
subTitle: Humpback whales may be in danger again as whaling nations explore new ways to re-open the hunt.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: A look at the marine mammals Manatees and Dugongs; each year, hundreds of these gentle giants' are killed in American waters by boats, disease and cold weather.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: Lionfish, a deadly Indo-Pacific species, have invaded the Caribbean and Atlantic, thanks to aquarium fish being released into the sea.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Bear the labrador has a sore leg, which turns into a really expensive problem; Mark travels down to Rotorua to help Stacey with a busy day on the farm.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Mark tries to save a girl's pet lamb; Stacey treats Max who has bad arthritis; Mark and Stacey volunteer for the day at SPCA.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Howdie and Dawn are working dogs and their owner wants them to mate, but neither are interested so Stacey intervenes; Mark treats a horse with a very sore leg; Stacey operates on Sushi the cat who has skin cancer.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: The red duiker thrives in the sub-tropical bushveld of the Kwa-Zulu Natal province; this sensitive animal will be captured in nets, gently removed and sent on their way to a new home.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: Traveling to the Entabeni Safari Conservancy where a family of African elephants awaits relocation; the family will be moved from a private conservation area as they have reached carrying capacity.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: With a high reproduction rate that leads to large numbers on reserves and are the staple diet for predators that keep their numbers in check; seeing how these highly adapted and unique animals get loaded and gently treated to migrate to a new home.

title: Modern Migrations
subTitle: Another member of Africa's Big Five, the white rhino; once on the brink of extinction, today thanks to the conservation and breeding strategies, there are enough to relocate to other nature reserves to ensure the survival of this unique species.

title: Gorillas: A Journey for Survival
subTitle: Four gorillas' emotional journey from South Africa into the Limbe Wildlife Centre in Cameroon.

subTitle: Indonesia's understaffed patrols from the Ministry of Maritime Affairs and Fisheries are having a hard time fighting the syndicate behind the extensive poaching.

subTitle: A deadly conflict exists between humans and baboons in the Western Cape Peninsula.

subTitle: The attempt to regrow a part of the African wilderness called the karoo.

subTitle: Sight, hearing, smell, touch, taste and beyond; survival demands an edge; these animals' abilities qualify them for nature's special forces.

subTitle: The lifecycle of the butterfly; how birds fly; how a Venus flytrap catches its prey.

subTitle: The jaguar's spots; how a bee sting works; salmon migrate thousands of miles to spawn.

subTitle: Ambulance drivers Sonia and Angela pick up a 50-kilo Dogue De Bordeaux who has a mysterious bed-wetting problem, which Cristina must try to understand; Lawrence is trying to find out why guinea pig Noel is feeling anxious and upset.

subTitle: Behind the scenes at the Blue Cross animal hospitals in London Victoria and Grimsby.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: A 17-year-old cat, a bulldog with a mystery swelling, newborn pups, and a mischievous puppy test the Blue Cross team's skill and compassion.

title: Arctic City
subTitle: The Arctic metropolis is home to hundreds of bears and over two thousand moose; but most surprising, are the city's five wild wolfpacks.

title: Arctic City
subTitle: Discover how people and animals cohabit in the remote urban metropolis that is Anchorage Alaska.

title: Arctic City
subTitle: Anchorage has a rather unique resident who is renowned as nature's lumberjack and North Americas largest rodent, the beaver.

title: Arctic City
subTitle: Anchorage is home to over 2000 moose and these notorious animals are running riot; with 300,000 people living alongside wild animals, the sizzling summer heat, sometimes brings conflict between humans and wildlife.

subTitle: When wielded precisely, teeth and claws are instruments for hunting, defense and combat in the animal world.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Jonathan visits Key Cork off the Belize coast at night to explore a famous ocean cave; they visit a cave called Hole in the Wall, located in a pond in Florida and accessible only by boat.

subTitle: With all the rain came an abundance of animals, to count them Rudie takes to the air; but only after saving a cheetah's life on the operating table.

subTitle: Takes a look at the hierarchy of animals in the wild as they fight each other to survive and try to emerge as the apex predator.

subTitle: The filmmakers shed light on how the Nile crocodile, the Gaboon viper and the great white shark survive using their fangs and teeth as weapons.
